verses.text: The LORD says: one more plague — and then he will let you go. When he does, he will thrust you out completely. Guidance: God names the end clearly. Before the worst hour comes, He tells Moses how it ends. Walk through this night with the morning already in view.

Speak to the people: let every man ask of his neighbor, and every woman of hers, jewels of silver and gold. The LORD gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moses himself was very great in their eyes — Pharaoh's servants and the common people both. Guidance: After centuries of unpaid labor, God arranges restitution before they leave. Generations of stolen wages do not go uncollected.

Speak to the people: let every man ask of his neighbor, and every woman of hers, jewels of silver and gold. The LORD gave the people favor in the sight of the Egyptians. Moses himself was very great in their eyes — Pharaoh's servants and the common people both. Guidance: After centuries of unpaid labor, God arranges restitution before they leave. Generations of stolen wages do not go uncollected.

Thus says the LORD: about midnight I will go out into the midst of Egypt. Every firstborn in Egypt shall die — from Pharaoh's heir on the throne to the firstborn of the maidservant at the mill — and all the firstborn of cattle. Guidance: God is no respecter of palaces. The judgment falls on every house, top to bottom — because every house participated in oppressing His own firstborn nation.

Thus says the LORD: about midnight I will go out into the midst of Egypt. Every firstborn in Egypt shall die — from Pharaoh's heir on the throne to the firstborn of the maidservant at the mill — and all the firstborn of cattle. Guidance: God is no respecter of palaces. The judgment falls on every house, top to bottom — because every house participated in oppressing His own firstborn nation.

There shall be a great cry throughout all Egypt, such as has never been and never will be again. Guidance: Sin's full bill, when it finally comes due, is not small. The cry of God's people held back for centuries finds its echo, in a single night, across all of Egypt.

But against any of the children of Israel, not a dog shall move his tongue — against man or beast. That you may know the LORD puts a difference between the Egyptians and Israel. Guidance: Even the dogs of Egypt will hold their voices. God's protection does not have to be loud to be total.

All your servants shall come down to me and bow themselves before me, saying: get out, you and all the people who follow you. And after that I will go out. Moses went out from Pharaoh in great anger. Guidance: Even prophets feel anger when faced with stubborn cruelty. The righteous response is not always serenity — sometimes it is fury that walks out of the room.

The LORD: Pharaoh will not listen, that My wonders may be multiplied in Egypt. Moses and Aaron did all these wonders before Pharaoh; the LORD hardened his heart, and he would not let Israel go. Guidance: Persistent refusal does not defeat God's plan — it becomes the canvas on which He paints more wonders. Even Pharaoh's no is being used.
